sub_pixel_system.notes: Puzzle game; sub-pixel movement system not documented

special_mechanics.name: Cursor-based Block Manipulation
special_mechanics.description: Player uses on-screen cursor to arrange and swap colored blocks. Clearing matching blocks (3+ same color horizontally or vertically) causes above blocks to fall, enabling chain reactions and combos.

physics_values.block_rise_rate: Steady rise increasing over time as stage progresses
physics_values.gravity_effect: Blocks above cleared lines fall downward
physics_values.chain_reaction: Blocks fall and can trigger cascades if matching adjacent blocks

special_mechanics.name: Block Stack Height Limit
special_mechanics.description: Game over triggered when blocks reach top of playfield. Blocks continuously generate from bottom and rise.

physics_values.playfield_height: Fixed boundary at top of screen

tier_notes: Tier 2: Tetris Attack is a puzzle game with no platformer movement mechanics. Sources provide gameplay descriptions and development history but contain no physics parameters, gravity values, jump mechanics, or sub-pixel system data. Movement mechanics are cursor-based puzzle manipulation rather than character locomotion.
sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Tetris Attack SNES article)
